disclaimer: Information may change. Always verify with official Chinese embassy or consulate before travel.
visaFreeNote: Check current policy as visa-free entry may be available for certain durations or purposes. Verify with official sources.

applicationSteps.title: Check current requirements
applicationSteps.detail: Visit the official Chinese Embassy in Japan website for the latest visa policies and forms.

applicationSteps.title: Gather required documents
applicationSteps.detail: Collect all necessary documents, ensuring they meet the specified criteria.

applicationSteps.title: Complete application form
applicationSteps.detail: Fill out the visa application form accurately and completely.

applicationSteps.title: Submit application
applicationSteps.detail: Submit your application in person at the embassy/consulate or through an authorized visa agency.

applicationSteps.title: Receive visa
applicationSteps.detail: Wait for processing and collect your passport with the visa, or arrange for delivery if available.

requiredDocuments: Valid passport with at least 6 months remaining validity and blank pages.
Completed China visa application form, signed and dated.
Recent passport-sized photo meeting embassy specifications.
Proof of accommodation (e.g., hotel booking or invitation letter from a host in China).
Round-trip flight itinerary showing entry and exit dates.
Bank statement from the past 3 months to demonstrate financial stability.
processingTime: Standard processing takes 4-7 business days; express services may be available for faster processing at an additional cost.
entryPorts: Major land border crossings like those with Hong Kong or Macau

customsRules.limit: Must declare if carrying more than $5,000 USD or equivalent in foreign currency.
Strictly no illegal drugs, weapons, explosives, or counterfeit goods.
Limits on items like cigarettes (e.g., 400 cigarettes duty-free), alcohol (e.g., 1.5 liters), and certain electronics may apply.
Generally allowed for personal use; high-value items may need declaration.

arrivalTips: Fill out the arrival/departure card provided on the plane before landing to save time at immigration.
Have your hotel address and contact information written in Chinese characters to show to immigration officials if needed.
Keep your passport, visa, and boarding pass easily accessible for inspection at immigration.
Be prepared to answer basic questions about your travel purpose, accommodation, and return plans.
Declare any items that exceed customs allowances at the red channel to avoid penalties.

usefulLinks.title: Chinese Embassy in Japan Official Website
